chemistry.reaction_name: Photocatalytic amide synthesis via acyloxyphosphonium ion formation
chemistry.scope_summary: Broad scope including aryl and aliphatic carboxylic acids, amino acids with various protecting groups, primary and secondary amines, drug molecules, and peptide synthesis including tetrapeptides via solid-phase synthesis

optimization_table.key_findings: PC-PF6 with cobaloxime A at 0.033 M gave 86% yield

figures.mechanism_type: photoredox catalysis for acyloxyphosphonium ion generation
key_observations_supporting_mechanism: Direct use of amine HCl salts
figures.caption: Scheme 1.  P-assisted deoxygenative substitutions.

Scheme 2.  Substrate scope. Reactions generally performed with photocatalyst  PC· PF 6  (1 mol%), cobaloxime  A  (5 mol%), carboxylic acid substrate (0.2 mmol), amine (0.3 mmol) and PPh 3  (1.0 equiv) in CH 2 Cl 2  (6 mL) under a nitrogen atmosphere and irradiation with blue LEDs for 12 h. Isolated yields are shown and enantiomeric excesses were determined by HPLC analysis using a chiral stationar
Scheme 3.  Synthetic applications. See Supporting Information for detailed procedures. Isolated yields. [a] The HCl salt of amine was used with NaHCO 3  (1.5 equiv).

Scheme 4.  Peptide synthesis. Peptide coupling conditions: 1.5 equivalent of NaHCO 3  was added for the reaction with HCl salt of amino acid ester. See Scheme 2 for general conditions and Supporting Information for specific experimental details. Deprotection: 15% Pd/C, CH 3 OH, rt, H 2 (1 atm). Conditions for SPPS:  PC· PF 6  (1 mol%), cobaloxime  A  (5 mol%), Fmoc-amino acid (1.0 equiv), PPh 3  (

key_observations_supporting_mechanism: TEMPO radical quenching experiment shows 0% yield of product 10
Competition experiments show alkene gives 72% yield vs 27% yield for pyridine product
Competition experiments show acrylate gives 73% yield vs 24% yield for benzyl ester
Calculated reduction potential E1/2calc = -1.31 V vs SCE for compound 99
Calculated reduction potential E1/2calc = -0.15 V vs SCE for acyl radical 101
figures.caption: Scheme 5.  Gram-scale synthesis in batch and continuous-flow. Reaction conditions: A mixture of carboxylic acid (1.0 equiv), amine (1.5 equiv), PC· PF 6  (1 mol%), cobaloxime  A  (5 mol%) and PPh 3  (1.0 equiv) was irradiated by blue LEDs. Reactions in batch and flow process were performed in 0.033 M and 0.06 M solution of CH 2 Cl 2 , respectively. All yields are isolated. [a] 0.5 mol%  PC· PF 6 . | Scheme 6.  Mechanistic insight.

figures.key_information: Amide coupling reaction setup showing carboxylic acids + amines → amides under standard conditions, with residence time of 40 min at 0.3 mL/min flow rate
figures.caption: Scheme 5.  Gram-scale synthesis in batch and continuous-flow. Reaction conditions: A mixture of carboxylic acid (1.0 equiv), amine (1.5 equiv), PC· PF 6  (1 mol%), cobaloxime  A  (5 mol%) and PPh 3  (1.0 equiv) was irradiated by blue LEDs. Reactions in batch and flow process were performed in 0.033 M and 0.06 M solution of CH 2 Cl 2 , respectively. All yields are isolated. [a] 0.5 mol%  PC· PF 6 . | Scheme 6.  Mechanistic insight.

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Long reaction times (12 hours) required for complete conversion
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Significant time reduction from 12 hours to 40 minutes residence time with higher concentration
translation_logic.what_changed: Residence time reduced from 720 min to 40 min, concentration increased from 0.033 M to 0.06 M
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Same catalysts, stoichiometry, solvent (CH2Cl2), light source (blue LEDs)
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Continuous processing reduces accumulation of reactive intermediates

conflicts.resolution: Used figure value (40 min) as higher fidelity source

notes: Well-documented photoredox catalysis study with clear batch-to-flow translation. Residence time conflict resolved in favor of figure data. High confidence due to comprehensive experimental details and mechanistic studies.
